title: silene multicaulis subsp. multicaulis
intro: Silene multicaulis subsp. multicaulis, also known as many-stemmed catchfly, is a flowering plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. The plant's medicinal properties make it beneficial for treating various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Silene multicaulis subsp. multicaulis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and its extracts have been used in traditional folk medicine to support overall health and well-being.
